segments.text: In the year that King Uzziah died, I saw the Lord sitting on a throne, high and lifted up, and the train of His robe filled the temple.
Above it stood seraphim; each one had six wings: with two he covered his face, with two he covered his feet, and with two he flew.
And one cried to another and said:
Holy, holy, holy is the Lord of hosts; The whole earth is full of His glory!
segments.speaker: Seraphim

segments.text: And the posts of the door were shaken by the voice of him who cried out, and the house was filled with smoke.
So I said:
Woe is me, for I am undone! Because I am a man of unclean lips, And I dwell in the midst of a people of unclean lips; For my eyes have seen the King, The Lord of hosts.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Then one of the seraphim flew to me, having in his hand a live coal which he had taken with the tongs from the altar.
And he touched my mouth with it, and said:
Behold, this has touched your lips; Your iniquity is taken away, And your sin purged.
segments.speaker: Seraphim

segments.text: Also I heard the voice of the Lord, saying:
Whom shall I send, And who will go for Us?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Here am I! Send me.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: And He said:
Go, and tell this people: 'Keep on hearing, but do not understand; Keep on seeing, but do not perceive.'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Make the heart of this people dull, And their ears heavy, And shut their eyes; Lest they see with their eyes, And hear with their ears, And understand with their heart, And return and be healed.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then I said:
Lord, how long?
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: And He answered:
Until the cities are laid waste and without inhabitant, The houses are without a man, The land is utterly desolate,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The Lord has removed men far away, And the forsaken places are many in the midst of the land.
But yet a tenth will be in it, And will return and be for consuming, As a terebinth tree or as an oak, Whose stump remains when it is cut down. So the holy seed shall be its stump.
